title: I'm not sure, I'm not familiar

messages.author: mowcius
messages.text: Most people using MeshCore are realistically not looking to create off-grid communication for any particular purpose. It's a hobby like any other. They want simple, they want to get a device, click some options and then connect to other people with the same hobby
messages.content_html: Most people using MeshCore are realistically not looking to create off-grid communication for any particular purpose. It's a hobby like any other. They want simple, they want to get a device, click some options and then connect to other people with the same hobby

messages.author: ded (SalishMesh)
messages.text: Most people setting up networks that I have encountered are doing exactly that. Building a resilient off grid alternative communication network
messages.content_html: Most people setting up networks that I have encountered are doing exactly that. Building a resilient off grid alternative communication network

messages.author: ded (SalishMesh)
messages.text: "This presentation by a Rectilium developer suggests that it does exactly that. And more." Always take this with caution, meshtastic makes a lot of claims too
messages.content_html: "This presentation by a Rectilium developer suggests that it does exactly that. And more." Always take this with caution, meshtastic makes a lot of claims too
messages.reactions_html: 1
messages.reply_html:
Backcaster
This presentation by a Rectilium developer suggests that it does exactly that. And more. https://media.ccc.de/v/38c3-reticulum-unstoppable-networks-for-the-people

messages.author: Backcaster
messages.text: Whilst still waiting for my T-Deck to arrive, so I may yet learn otherwise, I'm not sure I see any great difference in difficulty between flashing it for Rectilium/MeshCore, or if I was to flash a Heltec v3 as a companion node/rNode, between using the different messaging apps?
messages.content_html: Whilst still waiting for my T-Deck to arrive, so I may yet learn otherwise, I'm not sure I see any great difference in difficulty between flashing it for Rectilium/MeshCore, or if I was to flash a Heltec v3 as a companion node/rNode, between using the different messaging apps?
messages.reply_html:
mowcius
Most people using MeshCore are realistically not looking to create off-grid communication for any particular purpose. It's a hobby like any other. They want simple, they want to get a device, click some options and then connect to other people with the same hobby

messages.author: ded (SalishMesh)
messages.text: You should try both
messages.content_html: You should try both

messages.author: Backcaster
messages.text: I guess that's the difference between my IT background, and looking how I can leverage any technology, versus an enthusiast using it for fun?
messages.content_html: I guess that's the difference between my IT background, and looking how I can leverage any technology, versus an enthusiast using it for fun?
messages.reply_html:
mowcius
Look in the public channel (https://analyzer.letsme.sh/channels) and you'll see most messages are just hello, and people looking to see how far they can get and who's around

messages.author: ded (SalishMesh)
messages.text: Again, largely this is not enthusiasts and fun. We are actively building out a reliable network across huge areas
messages.content_html: Again, largely this is not enthusiasts and fun. We are actively building out a reliable network across huge areas

messages.author: mowcius
messages.text: I think that's a country specific thing
messages.content_html: I think that's a country specific thing

messages.author: ded (SalishMesh)
messages.text: Southern British Columbia to Eugene for example
messages.content_html: Southern British Columbia to Eugene for example

messages.author: ded (SalishMesh)
messages.text: Its happening in many places
messages.content_html: Its happening in many places
messages.reply_html:
mowcius
I think that's a country specific thing

messages.author: Backcaster
messages.text: But Rectilium can be flashed onto the same devices as MeshCore - https://github.com/markqvist/Reticulum/wiki/Frequently-Asked-Questions#rnode - so how is it too heavy?
messages.content_html: But Rectilium can be flashed onto the same devices as MeshCore - https://github.com/markqvist/Reticulum/wiki/Frequently-Asked-Questions#rnode - so how is it too heavy?
embeds_html:
Frequently Asked Questions
The cryptography-based networking stack for building unstoppable networks with LoRa, Packet Radio, WiFi and everything in between. - markqvist/Reticulum
messages.reply_html:
ded (SalishMesh)
I think people use this instead of reticulum because reticulum is too much, too heavy, too complicated this purpose. If you want to build a backup internet then maybe reticulum is better. But if you want simple reliable communication then meshcore

messages.author: ded (SalishMesh)
messages.text: But its reductive to claim meshcore is just for fun
messages.content_html: But its reductive to claim meshcore is just for fun

messages.author: mowcius
messages.text: Here in the UK (where we have the largest number of MeshCore nodes), it's clearly mostly a hobby for people. I can absolutely see how it can be useful as an emergency network - I'm not claiming it's just for fun
messages.content_html: Here in the UK (where we have the largest number of MeshCore nodes), it's clearly mostly a hobby for people. I can absolutely see how it can be useful as an emergency network - I'm not claiming it's just for fun

messages.author: ded (SalishMesh)
messages.text: It's an entire networking stack vs messaging only for one
messages.content_html: It's an entire networking stack vs messaging only for one
messages.reply_html:
Backcaster
But Rectilium can be flashed onto the same devices as MeshCore - https://github.com/markqvist/Reticulum/wiki/Frequently-Asked-Questions#rnode - so how is it too heavy?

messages.author: Backcaster
messages.text: It's certainly no big deal to flash both T-Deck and Seeed repeater so I can, albeit not both at the same time.

messages.author: Backcaster
messages.text: Can you explain more about problems with licence, please? Again, perhaps because my background is IT not ham radio, I thought that if it's over LoRa it's all much the same?

messages.author: Backcaster
messages.text: Ah, that kind of licence, thanks. I've just read the discussion on it here: https://github.com/markqvist/Reticulum/issues/850 It seems that the developer has a strong moral stance, wanting to ensure that his work is only used for good, and I can certainly respect that.
messages.content_html: Ah, that kind of licence, thanks. I've just read the discussion on it here: https://github.com/markqvist/Reticulum/issues/850 It seems that the developer has a strong moral stance, wanting to ensure that his work is only used for good, and I can certainly respect that.
embeds_html:
[Pseudo Pull-Request] - License Fixes · Issue #850 · markqvist/Re...
Note: This was originally supposed to be a PR but I don't think I actually have the legal right to contribute it that way so I am submitting it like this instead. Sorry for any inconvenience. I...
messages.reply_html:
Dayle - M0OUE
It changed from MIT to a custom one which caused a lot of arguments in the community. There's a few threads about it on the GitHub page
